description: Tracks schema changes per kryptonite version. Used by the validator to understand which fields existed at a given version and by migration logic to upgrade older epics.

changes.field: Phase A → A1 (parallel patch-gen) + A2 (serial apply)
changes.description: Phase A split: A1 generates patches in parallel in throwaway detached checkouts (write-only, no container/DB, identical in both execution modes); A2 applies them serially onto the mount via git am --3way. The two execution modes collapse into one pipeline with apply_target as the only switch. Verification (Phase B, all four gates against the integrated system) is unchanged — the integrated artifact is bit-identical to today's serial commit order. Deletes the old in-Phase-A per-file verify note. Adds a patchgen-* row to Worktree Cleanup Guarantees (G2) and an intra-group read-after-write prohibition (G1). Propagated the model through the story-status definition (merged = patch applied onto apply_target), the Execution-modes section, Phase B steps 1-2 (mode-guarded — no-op merge/remove in single_mounted_serial), and the cleanup table.

Cheaper fix loop, no gate weakened: orchestrator fixes TRIVIAL issues inline on the mount; service restarts batched once per attempt (health-check before any gate); code_review on subsequent attempts runs against the incremental fix diff only. UAT, UX, and spec_compliance ALWAYS re-run full against the integrated system — never scoped to a story↔file map.

createDetachedCheckout(repoPath, checkoutPath, ref) adds a branchless worktree for A1. applyPatch(repoPath, patchPath) applies a patch with git am --3way, distinguishes a true merge conflict (unmerged paths) from other am failures, and aborts the in-progress am so the orchestrator can re-dispatch the coder in rebase mode only on real conflicts. Covered by worktree-manager.test.js (node:test, 5 tests).

Worktree-mode report drops `branch` (checkouts are detached) and adds `patch_path` (git format-patch). The no-verification-claim rule now explicitly covers BOTH execution modes (A1 runs nothing in either). Adds a rebase-mode handling bullet for A2 patch conflicts. The coder report remains prompt-level (no JSON schema enforces it); patch_path is transient (checkout removed after A2) and is NOT persisted on the story object.

changes.field: A1/A2 loop + slim views + UX parallelism
changes.description: Per-wave loop rewritten for A1 parallel patch-gen → A2 serial apply. Each A1 coder gets a slim per-story view (never raw ~700KB state.json; gates still read raw state). Tools list gains createDetachedCheckout + applyPatch. Invariant 5 extended to detached checkouts. Phase B note: UX gate runs per-story compares in parallel.

The UX gate runs its per-story mock-vs-implementation compares in parallel (one Chrome MCP session per story). Safe because each compare is read-only render+screenshot+diff with no shared-DB-write surface — unlike UAT journey walks (write-dominated), whose parallelization was investigated and rejected for false-pass risk against the shared test DB.

Parallel-group grouping must never co-locate a true source dependency and its dependent in the same non-blocking group — A1 coders generate from the same base_sha and cannot read a sibling's not-yet-generated code. The dependency's owner goes in an earlier blocking group or shared_artifacts[].

notes: Behavioral/protocol change only — no epic data migration. 0.8.0 epics resume cleanly; the new Phase A pipeline produces the same integrated artifact the gates already expect.
No schema field is required. patch_path on the coder report is optional and prompt-level (no schema change shipped).

Two execution speedups were investigated and explicitly REJECTED (not shipped): parallel UAT journey walks (write-dominated journeys → false-pass against the shared test DB) and parallel fix-coders (failures cluster on shared surface; safety required a full re-verify the protocol forbids). See docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-29-phase12-speed.md 'Explicitly rejected'.
steps: Bump epic.json.kryptonite_version to '0.9.0' (optional — no gate depends on it).
No story/plan/spec changes required.

changes.description: Pre-dispatch reconciliation manifest. Each entry pins a model/graphql_type/graphql_enum/factory/base_class/migration that >=2 stories in the wave reference but that doesn't yet exist, so stories don't each invent an incompatible version. Fields: name, kind (enum), owner_story (the single creator, ^US-\d{3,}[a-z]?$), reused_by[] (consumers), canonical_representation (the agreed shape, e.g. the contested domain-noun resolution), repo. The owner goes in a blocking parallel group so it lands before reusers dispatch. Distinct from shared_registry_files (existing files many stories append to) — shared_artifacts is NEW shared surface created exactly once.

Enum worktree_parallel (default) | single_mounted_serial. worktree_parallel = one git worktree per story, coders in parallel (existing behavior). single_mounted_serial = work on the main mounted worktree, stories applied sequentially with serial verification — required when the dev env mounts only the main worktree into a container (sibling-worktree code is invisible to docker exec). Wave/story state machine is identical; only physical isolation differs. If a wave spans repos with different modes, orchestrator falls to single_mounted_serial for that wave.

New Phase 7.5 conventions slots for environment-assumption classes that bit Phase-12 execution wave-after-wave. schema_introspection_command: the command that dumps a NON-EMPTY schema (a naive dump can be empty when field visibility is scope-gated). compile_gate_command: dev-server-safe compile/typecheck (a full build can fail against a running dev-server container holding the output volume). test_db_setup: provision the shared test DB once per wave (some repos bootstrap via migrations, not schema:load; their db/schema.rb may be an empty sentinel). test_data_gotchas[]: project-specific factory traps fed to every Coder (test-side analogue of assertion_shapes). grep_gotchas[]: precise anchored patterns where a bare token grep would false-match prose/comments. test_runner.*.invocation guidance extended to recommend the container-qualified form (docker exec <stack>-<svc>-1 ...) since a fresh compose run container can lack private deps. All optional; project-supplied values, never shipped in the skill.

New semantic checks. ERRORS: shared_artifact_owner_missing (owner_story not in any wave), shared_artifact_owner_too_late (owner in a later wave than the declaring wave), shared_artifact_reuser_missing, shared_artifact_reuser_precedes_owner (a reuser cannot be in an earlier wave than its creator). WARNINGS (non-blocking): shared_artifact_owner_not_blocking (owner not in a blocking group), unreconciled_reference (a CamelCase namespaced ref like Foo::Bar appearing across >=2 stories with no shared_artifacts entry — single-story refs are skipped as likely external deps). Output gains a `warnings` array; exit code unchanged (warnings never fail).

New semantic check (WARNING). Fires when an entity is described account-less/global/shared/ownerless but carries a required, non-nullable owner reference (account_id/owner_id/etc.) — the latent contradiction (US-039/US-040 'library scenario') that stays hidden from Phase 8 until parallel Phase-12 coders invent incompatible representations. Output gains a `warnings` array; exit code unchanged.

No hard Phase-11.5 gate was added. Reconciliation is enforced by validate-plan.js consistency checks + the Plan Critic, by design: 'two stories share a concept' is not mechanically detectable from JSON, so a hard gate would pass on a lie — against the skill's discipline that a gate must never fake a pass.

changes.field: execution modes, blocking-group ordering, test-DB-once, serial verify
changes.description: New 'Execution modes' section (worktree_parallel vs single_mounted_serial). Phase A gains step 3.3 (provision shared test DB once before dispatch — never let parallel coders race the migrate), step 3.6 (dispatch blocking groups first and merge them before non-blocking groups), and dual-mode Phase A pseudocode. Phase B fix loop gains an explicit 'verify per-changed-file, serially — never the full wave spec set' rule. Worktree precondition relaxed to apply only to worktree_parallel repos.

changes.field: worktree-mode pass claims + invented-representation halt + per-file verify
changes.description: Worktree-mode reports MUST set tests_run to 'none (worktree mode)' and MUST NOT assert specs pass/rubocop clean — the wave gate is the sole source of truth (isolation hides cross-story bugs). Inventing a persistence/representation/identity shape the spec didn't define is now a mandatory NEEDS_CONTEXT halt (never DONE_WITH_CONCERNS). Fix-on-main verification reinforced to per-changed-file, never the whole suite.

changes.field: mode selection, blocking-first, test-DB-once, invented-representation escalation
changes.description: Per-wave loop documents execution-mode resolution, blocking-group-first dispatch, and provision-test-DB-once. Two new invariants (never dispatch non-blocking before blocking groups merge; never let two coders migrate the shared test DB concurrently) and a worktree-mode-report-can't-claim-pass clause on invariant 8. New escalation row: a Coder 'I invented a representation' NEEDS_CONTEXT halts the wave and is resolved in spec/shared_artifacts before re-dispatch.

New review dimension (section 8): for each wave, find shared concepts not captured in shared_artifacts[] — a domain noun two stories represent incompatibly, a model/type/enum >=2 stories race to create. Emit unreconciled_shared_concept critical_issues (NEEDS_REVISION). The judgment-call counterpart to validate-plan.js's mechanical checks.

changes.field: reconciliation step, convergence-cost heuristic, environment-assumption principle
changes.description: plan-assembly.md: shared_artifacts reconciliation step, owner-in-blocking-group rule, convergence-cost wave-sizing heuristic (prefer a blocking foundation sub-group then fan out leaves; resist max-width fan-out where stories converge on shared surface). spec-assembly.md: documents the data_model_ownership_conflict warning. SKILL.md Phase 7.5 + interviewer.md: the core principle that DOD validation commands encode environment assumptions which must be verified in Phase 7.5, not discovered at gate time.

notes: 0.7.0 epics resume cleanly. All new fields (shared_artifacts[], execution_mode, the new conventions slots) are optional — missing values mean previous behavior (worktree_parallel, no pre-wave reconciliation manifest).

The new validate-plan/validate-spec checks emit WARNINGS, never errors, for the heuristic cases (unreconciled_reference, data_model_ownership_conflict) — they cannot false-fail an older epic. The shared_artifacts consistency ERRORS only fire when a plan actually declares shared_artifacts[].
No new phase gate. Reconciliation is enforced by the Plan Critic + validate-plan.js, not a hard gate.
steps: Bump epic.json.kryptonite_version to '0.8.0'.
If a repo's dev env mounts only the main worktree into a container (so per-story worktrees can't be tested), set repos[].execution_mode = 'single_mounted_serial'.

During Phase 7.5, fill the new conventions slots where they apply: schema_introspection_command, compile_gate_command, test_db_setup, test_data_gotchas[], grep_gotchas[]. These are project values — not shipped in the skill.

During Phase 11, populate waves[].shared_artifacts[] for any wave where >=2 stories share a not-yet-existing model/type/enum/base-class, pinning one owner and the canonical_representation. Place owners in blocking parallel groups.

changes.description: has_mock added to top-level required[]. Phase 10 already required it — Phase 8 and the global schema treated it as optional with default false, causing 74-of-91 stories to clear Phase 8 and fail Phase 10 in field testing. Authoring (interviewer + designer) now sets it explicitly per story.
has_mock added to required[] (matches story-schema and Phase 10). The conditional `if has_mock then mock_phase + mock_approved` block kept; only the requiredness was aligned.

Pattern relaxed from ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$ to ^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*$. Phase 4 captured underscore-style party ids (account_admin, knowbe4_staff) which forced an undocumented remap during spec generation. No remap exists; party id passes through verbatim from epic.json to spec.json to stories[].party.

Harmonized and widened the chrome_mcp DOD step + plan user_journeys.steps[] action enums. Both now allow: navigate, click, fill, wait, wait_for, assert_text, assert_count, assert_visible, assert_not_visible, assert_url, assert_attribute, screenshot, resize_page, evaluate_script. Step objects also allow optional description/note keys for readability. story-schema.json gains $defs.chromeMcpStep as a single-source-of-truth definition; plan-schema mirrors the shape inline (kept in sync — divergence is the bug). Pre-0.7.0 enums omitted wait_for/resize_page/assert_not_visible/assert_attribute/evaluate_script and forbade description/note, forcing agents to drop legitimate validation steps (e.g. min-viewport gates) or normalize away their own readability keys.

Optional array of file paths this story modifies that are also modified by other stories in the same wave (registries, aggregator manifests, locale catalogs, GraphQL schema entry points). Listed here — not in the task's regular file_paths — so the parallel-group file-conflict check excludes them; the wave's shared_registry_files entry handles reconciliation at end-of-wave.

First-class concept for files multiple stories in a wave must each modify. Each entry: path, repo, kind (append | merge | regenerate), optional merge_strategy and regenerated_by. Excluded from the per-task file_conflict check; reconciled by end_of_wave_actions[]. Without this, every story touching a registry file forced a serial wave.

Codegen / registry-merge / schema-regeneration actions the orchestrator runs in the wave worktree AFTER all story merges but BEFORE Phase B begins. Each entry: name, command, optional repo, run_in (wave_worktree | main_worktree). Failure → wave.status = blocked, no fix loop (infrastructure-class). Documented in references/execution-protocol.md step 4.5.

Top-level array of human-gated prerequisites that block specific waves (Bedrock model access, LaunchDarkly flag creation, credential provisioning, manual data seeding). Each entry: id (PREFLIGHT-NNN), description, kind, owner, verification (executable check using the DOD validation methods), blocks_waves[]. Phase B step 0 runs verification; failure → wave.status = blocked. Lifted from plan.json into spec.json for visibility.

Phase 7.5 verified-repo-facts slot. Structured fields: app_root (container path from Dockerfile WORKDIR / docker-compose working_dir), test_runner.{backend,frontend,e2e} (each with name + invocation + config_path), directory_layout (open-ended map of artifact-type → relative path), assertion_shapes (open-ended per-area assertion shapes), verified_at (ISO timestamp). Populated by reading the repo, not asking the user. Coder agents must consult before writing files or composing test invocations; pre-0.7.0 epics burned hundreds of agent runs on wrong assumptions about app-root, test runner, and namespace layout.

Named map of repo-level regenerate commands (e.g. graphql_schema → 'rake graphql:schema:dump', db_schema → 'rake db:schema:dump'). Referenced by plan.waves[].end_of_wave_actions[] and plan.waves[].shared_registry_files[].regenerated_by so codegen is named once per repo and reused across waves.

Phase 7.5 gate (Repo Conventions Preflight). Base gate requires every epic-referenced repo to be present; 0.7.0+ supplemental requires conventions to be populated (app_root + at least one test_runner surface + non-empty directory_layout + verified_at). Pre-0.7.0 epics skip the supplemental — no retroactive enforcement.

Validator now accepts fractional --phase values (e.g. --phase 7.5 → loads 07_5.json). Filename slug for fractional phases swaps the dot for underscore so files sort lexically beside their integer siblings.

Optional third positional argument: <path-to-state.json>. When provided, validator asserts that for every story s in plan.waves[N].parallel_groups[*].stories, state.json.stories[s].wave === plan.waves[N].sequence. state.json is the source of truth for story.wave (see references/state-machine.md); the plan describes intent, the state describes execution.

Designer agent emits `design_notes` in its Report Format and state.json story field, matching references/story-schema.json. Pre-0.7.0 it emitted `direction_notes` while the schema accepted only `design_notes`, causing additionalProperties:false rejections.

New procedural references describing Phase 10 spec assembly (lift order, party-id passthrough, OQ→story mapping, synthesis-section subagent dispatch, Spec Critic loop) and Phase 11 plan assembly (wave-DAG layering via Kahn topological layering, parallel-group grouping with registry-file exclusion, critical-path derivation, conflict-safe file aggregation, Plan Critic loop). Pseudocode-level — there is intentionally no generate-spec.js / generate-plan.js script.

Replaced the absolutist 'mapper, not synthesizer' framing with two explicit categories: Lift sections (1:1 upstream slot in epic.json/state.json — ADRs, parties, NFRs, design system, stories, spike findings, risks, preflight_requirements, scope evolution) and Synthesis sections (architecture.components, architecture.interactions, data_model.entities, api_boundaries — derived from stories + ADRs by parallel subagents). Synthesis sections cannot be lifted; pretending they should be has caused real planning errors.

notes: 0.6.0 epics resume cleanly. The new gates and schema additions apply only to epics with epic.kryptonite_version >= 0.7.0 — older epics keep using the base schemas.
Phase 7.5 is a new phase. 0.6.0 epics skip it. 0.7.0+ epics must populate repos[].conventions before Phase 8 advances.

shared_registry_files, end_of_wave_actions, preflight_requirements, touches_registry_files, regenerate_commands are all additive — empty/missing values mean 'no special handling', which is the previous behavior.
steps: Bump epic.json.kryptonite_version to '0.7.0'.
Run Phase 7.5: for every repo this epic references, populate repos[].conventions by reading the repo (Dockerfile, docker-compose, package.json/Gemfile, directory scan). Confirm with the user.
If your project has registry/aggregator files multiple stories must each modify, declare them per-story (touches_registry_files[]) and per-wave (shared_registry_files[]) in the plan.
If your project has codegen files (GraphQL schema, db schema), declare repos[].regenerate_commands and reference them from plan.waves[].end_of_wave_actions[].

If your project has human-gated prerequisites (Bedrock model access, LaunchDarkly flags), move them from plan.risks[] (where they were a stretch) into plan.preflight_requirements[] with a real verification check.
If your designer agent emitted direction_notes in pre-0.7.0 state.json, rename to design_notes (it's the same value; only the key changes).

changes.description: Phase 3 architecture decisions captured as ADRs. Same shape as spec-schema.json's architecture.decisions[], plus extension fields source_phase and related_stories[] for traceability. Lifted into spec.json.architecture.decisions[] verbatim during Phase 10 (the generator drops the extension fields).
Phase 3 unresolved gaps captured as OQs. Same shape as spec-schema.json's open_questions[], plus extension field source_phase. Lifted into spec.json.open_questions[] verbatim during Phase 10.
Phase 6 append-only log of scope deltas after spike findings. Each entry records phase, trigger, and added/removed/modified/deferred story IDs.
New JSON schema defining the full epic.json shape. Replaces the inline field list previously in storage-protocol.md (which had drifted from reality).

Phase 7 output. Was a free-form object with minProperties: 1 — now structured under testing/non_functional/infrastructure/patterns. Phase 7 gate (on 0.6.0+ epics) requires at least one of the four sub-objects to be populated.

Phase 8 output. Now a structured object (nav, header, layout, colors, typography, spacing, components) instead of free-form prose. Phase 8 gate (on 0.6.0+ epics) requires shell_summary to be populated whenever design_direction.locked === true so detail-mock Designers inherit the visual DNA deterministically.

Version-suffixed supplemental gate schemas. validate-gate.js loads them on top of the base gate when epic.kryptonite_version >= 0.6.0. Older epics keep using the base gate alone — no retroactive enforcement.

Validator now loads version-suffixed supplemental gate schemas (e.g. 03.0.6.0.json) on top of the base gate when the epic's kryptonite_version is at or above the suffix. Errors from supplementals are labeled SCHEMA (vX.Y.Z+) for clarity.

Canonicalizes the per-story mock working-data the Phase 8 mock workflow already writes into state.json. mock_path (string, repo-relative path to the approved mock HTML — already emitted by agents/designer.md's Report Format and already allowed by wave-gate-report-schema.json), mock_options (array of option identifiers shown in /compare, e.g. ['a','b','c']), mock_choice (string, the selected option), design_notes (string, free-form notes on the chosen content-area treatment, inherited by detail Designers and the spec generator). BUGFIX: story-schema.json sets additionalProperties:false, so before this entry these four fields failed the Phase 9 gate even though the skill's own designer agent produces them — an internal inconsistency. No migration needed: the fields were already present in 0.6.0 epics' state.json and are now simply permitted.

notes: 0.5.0 epics resume cleanly. The new gates apply only to epics created on 0.6.0+ — the validator keys off epic.kryptonite_version, not the installed plugin version.
An in-flight 0.5.0 epic finishes Phase 8 on its current shape. To bring an old epic onto the 0.6.0 schema, follow the optional migration mapping below.
steps: If the epic has a sidecar gap_analysis.md (or equivalent Phase 3 notes), translate each confirmed gap-probe resolution into an ADR under epic.json.decisions[] (id ADR-001+, status 'accepted', context/choice/consequences/alternatives per references/epic-schema.json). Translate each unresolved gap into an OQ under epic.json.open_questions[].
If the epic has a sidecar rescope.md, translate each scope delta into an entry under epic.json.scope_history[] with phase, trigger, and added/removed/modified/deferred arrays.

If the epic has a sidecar technical_guidance.md, translate the test approach into technical_context.testing.{backend,frontend,e2e}; non-functional sections into technical_context.non_functional.*; infrastructure integrations into technical_context.infrastructure.{integrate_with,do_not_integrate,gating}; architectural patterns into technical_context.patterns.{honor,avoid}.

If foundational mocks are approved, write the design system summary into epic.json.design_direction.shell_summary as a structured object (nav, header, layout, colors, typography, spacing, components).
Bump epic.json.kryptonite_version to '0.6.0'. The next phase gate run will exercise the new supplemental schemas.
Rename any migrated sidecar markdowns to *.legacy.md so the interviewer doesn't re-read them as authoritative — they're paper trail only.

changes.description: Required array of user journeys per wave. Each journey has id (UJ-NNN), name, stories_covered[], and steps[] (Chrome MCP actions). Used by wave-uat-agent.
Optional top-level config to toggle individual gates and tune max_fix_attempts and fix_strategies.

Optional structured block: start_command, stop_command, health_check, app_url, ready_signal, startup_timeout_ms. Drives service lifecycle in Phase B. Without it, UAT/UX gates skip for that repo's stories.
ISO timestamp of when the story branch was merged into the wave branch.
Per-story validation fields dropped — wave-level gates replace per-story QA/Reviewer/CodeReview agents.

From [pending, in_progress, qa_validation, in_review, done, blocked, cancelled, deferred] to [pending, in_progress, merged, done, blocked, cancelled, deferred]. Story is 'merged' after its branch lands in the wave branch; promotes to 'done' when the wave completes.

Replaces freeform spec.html. Validated by references/spec-schema.json. Composes story-schema.json via $ref. Contains overview, parties, repos, architecture (components/interactions/decisions), data_model, api_boundaries, nfrs, technical_constraints, design_direction, stories, spike_findings, open_questions.
Replaces freeform plan.html. Validated by references/plan-schema.json. Contains summary, waves (with parallel_groups, tasks, demo_checkpoint, user_journeys), critical_path, parallel_strategy, risks.

Wave gate reports have status [pass, fail, blocked]. Blocked = infrastructure unavailable; orchestrator pauses for user instead of entering fix loop. Issues[].severity gains 'blocked' alongside critical/high/medium/low.
Immutable history of wave gate attempts. Each entry has uat/ux/spec_compliance/code_review status + report_path, plus issues[] with fix_attempts[] for adaptive retry tracking.

steps: BREAKING: 0.4.0 only supports the wave-gate execution model. Projects mid-execution under 0.3.0 will not run on 0.4.0 — finish them on 0.3.0 or restart Phase 12 under 0.4.0.
Update any story with status 'qa_validation', 'in_review', or 'code_review' to 'merged' (or 'in_progress' if work hasn't started). The new schema rejects the old values.
Drop the per-story fields dod_validation, review_status, code_review_status, qa_status if present — they are no longer recognized.
Convert spec.html to spec.json (validated against references/spec-schema.json) and plan.html to plan.json (validated against references/plan-schema.json). The new file format is required.
Add a user_journeys[] array to each wave in plan.json with at least one journey covering the wave's stories — the plan validator rejects waves without it.
Recommended: add a 'testing' block to each repo in repos.json that needs a running service. Without it, UAT and UX gates skip for that repo (with warnings).

changes.description: Static frontend: DaisyUI + Tailwind + Alpine.js dark mode UI with Overview, Stories (kanban), Mocks (gallery+compare), Dashboard pages
API endpoint returning epic.json content
API endpoint returning repos.json as array
Epic switcher — list all epics and switch active context without restart
Migrated from flat array to { spec_comments: [], story_comments: [] } with threading (parent_id) and resolve toggle
Phase 12 semantic check: stories in an in_progress wave must not be 'pending' or missing status

steps: comments.json: if file is a plain array, it becomes { spec_comments: <array>, story_comments: [] } — handled automatically on load
Stories in active waves should have status updated from 'pending' to 'in_progress' when wave execution begins

changes.description: Version stamp for migration tracking
Two-phase mock system: foundational vs detail
Structured design system summary from foundational mocks
Records which mock phase locked the direction
List of story IDs classified as foundational mocks
List of story IDs classified as detail mocks
Phase gate validation schemas (01-12)

steps: Add kryptonite_version: '0.2.0' to epic.json
For stories with has_mock: true, add mock_phase: 'detail' (safe default — assumes direction was already established)
